evidence: WordSketchApiServer constructor directly instantiates SemanticFieldExplorer via 'this.semanticFieldExplorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor)' rather than accepting it as an injected dependency — creates a hidden constructor-time dependency that cannot be overridden without subclassing (WordSketchApiServer.java line 67)

SemanticFieldExplorer has a string-path constructor that opens a BlackLabIndex (new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath)) internally, but its own Javadoc warns: 'Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) for testability and to avoid double-open of the same index (see issue 297b2b52)' — yet WordSketchApiServer's DI constructor still creates a concrete SemanticFieldExplorer, bypassing the recommended injection path (SemanticFieldExplorer.java lines 93-98)
BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor performs blocking I/O (BlackLab.open) and declares throws IOException — this is explicit and properly surfaced, not a hidden import-time side effect
HttpServer is created and routes registered inside start(), not the constructor — a valid lazy-init pattern; no boot-order issue here
No module-level or static-initializer side effects found (no static { } blocks, no env reads at class load time, no DB connections at field initialization)
initialization_coupling.issues_preventing_higher_score: WordSketchApiServer hard-wires SemanticFieldExplorer construction in its constructor, making the explorer non-injectable and preventing test isolation without modifying production code.

strengths: BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor explicitly declares throws IOException for its index-open I/O, so callers cannot accidentally ignore the initialization cost or failure mode
HttpServer creation is deferred to start(), correctly separating object construction from server lifecycle
No static initializer side effects, environment variable reads at class load time, or circular import dependencies found anywhere in the codebase
initialization_coupling.issue_character: The single initialization coupling defect is localized: WordSketchApiServer hardwires a concrete SemanticFieldExplorer in its constructor, blocking injection and contradicting the SEF's own documentation recommending DI for testability.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: The codebase follows solid initialization discipline overall: I/O is explicit, servers start lazily, and there are no import-time side effects. The one defect is that WordSketchApiServer constructs SemanticFieldExplorer internally rather than accepting it via injection, which means test code must construct a live executor or subclass the server to override it. This is a real but contained issue — it affects one class's testability, not boot-order reliability across the application. That places the score at 80: solid but with one recurring friction point a developer would hit when writing integration tests.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer constructs SemanticFieldExplorer directly in its constructor, blocking injection and contradicting SEF's own DI recommendation
evidence: WordSketchApiServer constructor line 67: 'this.semanticFieldExplorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor)' — SemanticFieldExplorer is created unconditionally from the injected executor

SemanticFieldExplorer.java lines 93-98 Javadoc: 'Prefer SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) (dependency injection) for testability and to avoid double-open of the same index' — the recommended approach is injection, but WordSketchApiServer bypasses this by constructing directly
Tests that want to inject a mock SemanticFieldExplorer cannot do so without modifying WordSketchApiServer or subclassing it
issues.suggestion: Add a second WordSketchApiServer constructor that accepts SemanticFieldExplorer as a parameter: 'public WordSketchApiServer(QueryExecutor executor, String indexPath, int port, GrammarConfigLoader grammarConfig, SemanticFieldExplorer sef)'. Have the existing 4-arg constructor delegate to it by calling 'new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor)' as the default. This preserves backward compatibility while enabling injection in tests.

root_causes: SemanticFieldExplorer was added to WordSketchApiServer during feature development without wiring it through the existing constructor injection pattern used for QueryExecutor and GrammarConfigLoader
